query: I'm writing an immediate-mode GUI and my hover highlight flickers between two overlapping panels — how should I decide which control the mouse is over?
rationale: direct: immediate-mode occlusion/hover resolved via frame delay (last-drawn wins)

query: our editor UI pushes tens of thousands of rectangles to the GPU every frame and it's bandwidth-bound — how do I get the whole UI down to one draw call?
rationale: direct: primitive buffers + index encoding + single draw call

query: how do I add Tab/Shift-Tab keyboard navigation and 'first responder' style focus to an IMGUI that has no widget tree?
rationale: direct: responder chain, scope stack, event trickling, tab order

query: I want to drag an item from one panel and drop it onto another panel in my tool — what's the cleanest way to track the drag across windows?
rationale: implicit: data-model-first drag-and-drop, global drag id, cross-window

rationale: implicit: per-monitor DPI, virtual coords, DPI-keyed font atlas

query: two of my list rows share the same hover state and interaction jumps between them — how should controls be identified each frame?
rationale: implicit: stable unique ids, id-stack scoping, no position-derived ids
